prompt: Ops turned on `-W error` for every service in the depot fleet last week ("warnings are tomorrow's outages", fine) and the parcel-stamp library promptly stopped passing its gate, `python3 -W error test_parcelstamp.py`. It dies the moment the suite starts — doesn't even get as far as actually stamping anything — and once you get past THAT there's apparently more of the same lurking behind it; on my machine I peeled one layer off and hit another. The crew's little self-check script (checks.py, the thing they run after editing routing tables) has also been broken for a while on newer Python and nobody got around to it. Nothing about the library's actual behavior should change: same stamp lines, same pickup windows, same postal validation verdicts, and the carrier context still has to verify certs+hostname and refuse anything under TLS 1.2 — the gate test pins all of that. Please modernize whatever the interpreter is complaining about until the gate is green. test_parcelstamp.py is frozen; everything else in the folder is ordinary code.
notes: 5W1 item 4: -W error DeprecationWarning sweep, CPython 3.13.11 (all emission points probed on this box). Layered failures: (1) postal.py invalid regex escapes ("\d", "\s") — SyntaxWarning at module compile, fatal at import under -W error, so the gate dies before running anything (the 'dies the moment the suite starts' symptom); regex behavior identical after raw-string fix. (2) datetime.utcnow() x2 in stamps.py — DeprecationWarning at call time; fix datetime.now(UTC); tests pin format/window-span so naive->aware stays behavior-identical. (3) ssl: SSLContext(PROTOCOL_TLS) and options|=OP_NO_* both warn at call time (probed — mere attribute access does NOT warn on 3.13); fix PROTOCOL_TLS_CLIENT + minimum_version=TLSv1_2; test pins check_hostname/CERT_REQUIRED/minimum_version>=TLSv1_2, which the old context also fails behaviorally (MINIMUM_SUPPORTED). (4) unittest aliases: catalog says assertEquals as DeprecationWarning, but the aliases were REMOVED in 3.12 — on this box it surfaces as AttributeError when the crew self-check runs (documented deviation; the modernization fix is identical: assertEqual). Per catalog, the aliases live in checks.py, an in-repo dev helper that is NOT protected; the gate test runs it in-process via unittest loader and requires wasSuccessful. Baseline fails deterministically at import of postal.
